touches.ai_summary: Inbound request received from Caleb Harris at RubyMedia expressing interest in call recording and coaching solutions for their sales team. Initial contact captured via web form submission.
next_steps: Schedule qualification call with Caleb

touches.ai_summary: Qualification call with Caleb Harris to understand current state and pain points. RubyMedia currently lacks visibility into sales conversations and coaching is ad-hoc. Team of 22 sales reps across e-commerce vertical. Caleb mentioned they're also evaluating Chorus.

questions_asked.question: What's driving you to look for a call recording solution now?
questions_asked.answer: We've scaled to 22 reps and managers can't effectively coach without visibility into actual calls

questions_asked.question: What kind of integrations do you support with our CRM?
questions_asked.answer: We have native integrations with all major CRMs including Salesforce and HubSpot

next_steps: Send product overview deck
Schedule discovery call for next week

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email sent with product overview deck and case study from similar e-commerce company. Highlighted coaching analytics and unlimited automation features.
next_steps: Confirm discovery call scheduled for Thursday

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email confirming discovery call details. Caleb responded asking to include Charlotte Wu from procurement in future conversations.

questions_asked.question: Can we include our Sales Manager Charlotte Wu in the next call?
questions_asked.answer: Absolutely, I'll send a calendar invite to both of you

next_steps: Update calendar invite to include Charlotte Wu

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up call with Caleb to prepare for deeper discovery. Discussed current tech stack and integration requirements. Caleb shared that Chorus demo is scheduled for same week.

questions_asked.question: Walk me through your current sales process and tools
questions_asked.answer: We use Salesforce, Outreach for cadences, and Zoom for calls but no recording or analytics

questions_asked.question: How does your pricing compare to Chorus?
questions_asked.answer: We're generally competitive and I can provide detailed pricing once we understand your full requirements

next_steps: Deep-dive discovery call scheduled with expanded agenda

touches.ai_summary: Discovery call focused on pain points and current coaching process. Caleb described challenges with rep ramp time and inconsistent messaging. Currently using manual call reviews which is time-intensive for managers.

questions_asked.question: How long does it currently take to ramp new sales reps?
questions_asked.answer: About 4-5 months to full productivity, longer than we'd like

questions_asked.question: What does your current coaching process look like?
questions_asked.answer: Managers try to listen to a few calls per week per rep but it's inconsistent and time-consuming

questions_asked.question: Can your system automatically flag competitive mentions?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we can create custom trackers for competitor names and key topics

next_steps: Schedule technical evaluation session
Send ROI calculator based on ramp time improvement

touches.ai_summary: Extended discovery call diving into specific use cases: onboarding new reps, manager coaching workflows, and deal risk identification. Caleb expressed strong interest in automated coaching features and analytics dashboards.

questions_asked.question: Who would be the primary users of the coaching analytics?
questions_asked.answer: Our Sales Managers initially, but we want reps to have self-service access too

questions_asked.question: What metrics matter most to your sales leadership?
questions_asked.answer: Talk-to-listen ratio, question quality, objection handling, and competitive positioning

questions_asked.question: How customizable are the coaching scorecards?
questions_asked.answer: Fully customizable - you can create your own criteria based on your sales methodology

next_steps: Technical eval session with IT to review security and integrations
Provide custom demo focused on coaching workflows

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up call to align on technical requirements before eval session. Discussed data security, recording storage, and compliance needs. Caleb mentioned leadership hasn't been deeply involved yet but expects they will be before final decision.

questions_asked.question: Any specific security or compliance requirements we should address?
questions_asked.answer: Standard SOC 2 and GDPR compliance, nothing unusual for our industry

questions_asked.question: What's the implementation timeline typically look like?
questions_asked.answer: Usually 2-3 weeks from contract signature to full rollout for your team size

next_steps: Confirm technical eval attendees
Prepare security documentation

touches.ai_summary: Technical evaluation session with Caleb and IT team covering integrations, security architecture, data retention policies, and API capabilities. IT team asked detailed questions about call recording storage and Salesforce sync. Generally positive technical validation.

questions_asked.question: Where is call recording data stored and for how long?
questions_asked.answer: Stored in SOC 2 compliant AWS infrastructure, retention configurable from 90 days to unlimited based on your policy

questions_asked.question: How real-time is the Salesforce sync for call data?
questions_asked.answer: Near real-time, typically 2-3 minute lag for call summary and metadata to appear in Salesforce

questions_asked.question: Any integration concerns with your current tech stack?
questions_asked.answer: No major concerns, we use standard tools and your integrations look solid

questions_asked.question: Can we control which calls get recorded and which don't?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, configurable by user, team, or call type with manual override options

next_steps: Schedule product demo for Caleb and sales managers
Provide technical documentation for IT review

touches.ai_summary: Product demo focused on coaching workflows and manager analytics. Walked through conversation intelligence features, automated scorecards, and trend analysis. Caleb engaged throughout and asked about scaling to more users in future.

questions_asked.question: If we wanted to expand beyond 22 seats later, how does pricing scale?
questions_asked.answer: Volume discounts kick in at 50+ seats, and we can structure flex pricing for planned growth

questions_asked.question: Which features resonated most with your use case?
questions_asked.answer: The automated coaching scorecards and competitive intelligence tracking are exactly what we need

questions_asked.question: How does this compare feature-wise to Chorus?
questions_asked.answer: Very similar core capabilities, our differentiation is in the unlimited automation and more flexible coaching frameworks

next_steps: Prepare pricing proposal for 22 seats with unlimited automation tier
Schedule follow-up to discuss business case

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email summarizing demo highlights and sending ROI analysis based on reduced ramp time and improved win rates. Attached case study from similar-sized e-commerce company showing 35% ramp time reduction.
next_steps: Schedule pricing discussion
Align on decision timeline and stakeholders

touches.ai_summary: Executive meeting to discuss business case and ROI. Caleb brought Charlotte Wu but no VP-level sponsor yet. Focused conversation on productivity gains and coaching efficiency. Charlotte asked about contract terms and budget approval process.

questions_asked.question: Who else needs to be involved in the final decision?
questions_asked.answer: Charlotte and I can approve up to $25K, above that we need VP Sales sign-off

questions_asked.question: What's your standard contract length?
questions_asked.answer: Annual contracts are standard, with option for multi-year with additional discount

questions_asked.question: What does implementation support look like?
questions_asked.answer: Dedicated CSM for onboarding, training sessions, and 90-day check-ins included

next_steps: Send formal pricing proposal
Identify VP Sales for executive alignment

touches.ai_summary: Pricing call to review proposal. Presented $19,200 ACV for 22 seats on unlimited automation tier. Charlotte pushed back on price relative to Chorus quote they received. Discussion around contract flexibility and payment terms.

questions_asked.question: Can you break down the pricing difference between tiers?
questions_asked.answer: Standard tier is $650/seat with limited automation, unlimited tier is $873/seat with full feature access

questions_asked.question: What pricing did Chorus provide for comparison?
questions_asked.answer: They quoted around $17K but on a more limited feature set

objections: Price is higher than Chorus quote we received
next_steps: Review pricing options and potential discount scenarios
Schedule executive alignment meeting with VP Sales

touches.ai_summary: Executive meeting attempted but VP Sales was not able to attend. Caleb and Charlotte discussed budget constraints and competitive pressure from Chorus pricing. Need to demonstrate clear value differentiation to justify price premium.

questions_asked.question: What would make this an easy yes for your VP?
questions_asked.answer: Clearer ROI justification and pricing closer to Chorus would help

questions_asked.question: Is there any flexibility on the pricing you showed?
questions_asked.answer: Let me discuss with leadership - we may have room to move for the right partnership

objections: VP Sales hasn't been engaged enough to champion this purchase
next_steps: Reschedule VP meeting
Explore pricing flexibility internally

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email proposing revised pricing structure with slight discount and emphasizing unlimited automation value versus Chorus limitations. Requested confirmed meeting time with VP Sales.
next_steps: Await VP Sales availability
Prepare executive business case deck

touches.ai_summary: Negotiation call with Caleb and Charlotte to discuss pricing flexibility. Proposed 8% discount bringing total to $17,664 ACV. Charlotte indicated this is more competitive but still needs VP approval and wants to finalize Chorus comparison.

questions_asked.question: Is this your best and final pricing?
questions_asked.answer: This is the strongest discount we can offer while maintaining the unlimited automation tier

questions_asked.question: What's your timeline for making a decision?
questions_asked.answer: Need to decide within next two weeks, budget cycle deadline approaching

next_steps: Send revised proposal at $17,664
Confirm VP Sales meeting by end of week

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email with revised proposal at $17,664 ACV and updated ROI calculator. Included competitive comparison document highlighting unlimited automation advantage. Caleb acknowledged receipt and said working to schedule VP meeting.
next_steps: Wait for VP meeting confirmation
Prepare for potential final negotiation

touches.ai_summary: Final pricing call with Caleb to address last questions before VP meeting. Caleb indicated they're leaning toward our solution but VP wants to understand why price is still above Chorus. Discussed payment terms options (quarterly vs annual prepay).

questions_asked.question: Would quarterly payments be possible instead of annual upfront?
questions_asked.answer: We can offer quarterly payments with a small 3% upcharge, bringing total to $18,194

questions_asked.question: How is the Chorus evaluation progressing on your end?
questions_asked.answer: They're competitive on price but the feature gaps you've highlighted are real concerns for our use case

next_steps: Provide quarterly payment option in writing
